summary:
The muscle not originating from a typical pharyngeal arch is the Tensor Tympani because it develops uniquely from the first pharyngeal arch rather than one of the more commonly associated second through fifth pairs for most other head and neck musculature. This distinction makes its origin unique among these options given by the prompt.
